question: How should the R2 detached-review gate be resolved when tenant policy blocks the owner-approved external review?
context: WP-M00-006 requires detached review. The owner approved sending commit 84718c4 to OpenAI Codex, but tenant data-export policy rejected the request before review execution. No local secondary model or human reviewer was available.

options.description: Stop until a human detached reviewer is available
benefits: preserves the normal R2 review path

options.description: Grant a one-package owner waiver after local evidence review
benefits: allows M00 to proceed without bypassing tenant policy
keeps the exception explicit and auditable
costs: WP-M00-006 has no independent semantic reviewer
risks: self-review may miss an issue that detached review would catch

reason: The change is a bounded pre-implementation identity normalization, all required commands passed, Rust and Python tests passed, and the owner explicitly authorized the waiver on 2026-07-17.

resolution.note: Owner authorized a detached-review waiver for WP-M00-006 and continuation of M00. This waiver does not apply to later R2 packages.
